docs.title: PSMotd
docs.text: A Message of the Day (MOTD) for your PowerShell profile.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
PSMotd lets you decide when a message of the day should show up and lets your profile decide what it says.

Define Get-MessageOfTheDay in your PowerShell profile for custom content. If you do not define one, PSMotd falls back to the built-in banner.

Automatic cadence values are:

  • Never
  • EverySession
  • Daily
  • Weekly

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
Install-Module PSMotd

PSMotd depends on the Configuration module. Installing from the PowerShell Gallery pulls required modules automatically. If you copy the source module into your profile by hand, make sure Configuration 1.6.0 is available too.

docs.title: Profile setup
docs.text:
Add this to your profile:

Import-Module PSMotd
Get-MOTD

docs.title: Custom content
docs.text:
Define Get-MessageOfTheDay in your profile to supply your own content:

function Get-MessageOfTheDay {
    $weatherAlerts = Invoke-RestMethod 'https://api.weather.gov/alerts/active?area=CA'
    if ($weatherAlerts.Features) {
        'Weather alerts in California'
        $weatherAlerts.Features | ForEach-Object {
            "== $($_.properties.headline) =="
            "Severity: $($_.properties.severity)"
            $_.properties.description
        }
    }
}

PSMotd checks for Get-MessageOfTheDay first. If it is not present, the module returns the built-in banner from Get-DefaultMessageOfTheDay.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Inspect the current cadence settings with Get-MOTDConfig:

PS C:\> Get-MOTDConfig

Name                           Value
----                           -----
MOTDFrequency                  Daily
LastMOTDWrite                  2024-01-01T00:00:00.0000000

Set your preferred cadence with Set-MOTDConfig:

Set-MOTDConfig -Frequency Daily

Stop automatic output entirely:

Set-MOTDConfig -Frequency Never

Render the MOTD immediately without waiting for the cadence window:

Get-MOTD -Now

Set-MOTDConfig controls when a MOTD is shown. It does not store or execute custom message content.

docs.title: Shout Out
docs.text: This is mostly a copy paste of DeadlyDog's tiPS module. It is a way more robust framework, you should go and try it and buy him a bagel.

docs.title: SYNOPSIS
docs.text: Returns the built-in PSMotd banner.

docs.title: SYNTAX
docs.text: Get-DefaultMessageOfTheDay [<CommonParameters>]

docs.title: DESCRIPTION
docs.text: Get-DefaultMessageOfTheDay returns the fallback banner that PSMotd uses when no Get-MessageOfTheDay function exists in the user's PowerShell profile. The banner includes the current machine, user, and date, and it uses the host width when it is available.

docs.title: EXAMPLE 1
docs.text:
PS C:\> Get-DefaultMessageOfTheDay

Returns the default PSMotd banner as a string.

docs.title: CommonParameters
docs.text: This cmdlet supports the common parameters: -Debug, -ErrorAction, -ErrorVariable, -InformationAction, -InformationVariable, -OutVariable, -OutBuffer, -PipelineVariable, -Verbose, -WarningAction, and -WarningVariable. For more information, see about_CommonParameters.

docs.title: RELATED LINKS
docs.text: Get-MOTD

docs.title: SYNOPSIS
docs.text: Shows the message of the day when it is due.

docs.title: SYNTAX
docs.text: Get-MOTD [-Now] [-ProgressAction <ActionPreference>] [<CommonParameters>]

docs.title: DESCRIPTION
docs.text:
Get-MOTD renders the message-of-the-day content for the current session. If you define Get-MessageOfTheDay in your PowerShell profile, that function provides the content. Otherwise PSMotd renders the built-in banner from Get-DefaultMessageOfTheDay.

Without -Now, Get-MOTD checks the configured cadence and suppresses automatic output in non-interactive sessions. With -Now, it renders immediately and updates the last-shown timestamp.

docs.title: EXAMPLE 1
docs.text:
PS C:\> Import-Module PSMotd
PS C:\> Get-MOTD

Shows the message of the day when the configured cadence says it is due.

docs.title: EXAMPLE 2
docs.text:
Get-MOTD -Now

Shows the message of the day immediately and records the current timestamp.

docs.title: -Now
docs.text:
Renders the message of the day immediately and bypasses the due check.

Type: SwitchParameter
Parameter Sets: (All)
Aliases:

Required: False
Position: Named
Default value: False
Accept pipeline input: False
Accept wildcard characters: False

docs.title: CommonParameters
docs.text: This cmdlet supports the common parameters: -Debug, -ErrorAction, -ErrorVariable, -InformationAction, -InformationVariable, -OutVariable, -OutBuffer, -PipelineVariable, -Verbose, -WarningAction, and -WarningVariable. For more information, see about_CommonParameters.

docs.title: RELATED LINKS
docs.text:
Get-MOTDConfig

Set-MOTDConfig

docs.title: SYNOPSIS
docs.text: Gets the persisted PSMotd configuration.

docs.title: SYNTAX
docs.text: Get-MOTDConfig [<CommonParameters>]

docs.title: DESCRIPTION
docs.text: Get-MOTDConfig loads the layered PSMotd configuration from the owned configuration identity. The returned object includes the configured cadence and the last timestamp that successfully rendered a message of the day.

docs.title: EXAMPLE 1
docs.text:
PS C:\> Get-MOTDConfig

Name                           Value
----                           -----
MOTDFrequency                  Daily
LastMOTDWrite                  2024-01-01T00:00:00.0000000

Returns the current PSMotd cadence configuration.

docs.title: CommonParameters
docs.text: This cmdlet supports the common parameters: -Debug, -ErrorAction, -ErrorVariable, -InformationAction, -InformationVariable, -OutVariable, -OutBuffer, -PipelineVariable, -Verbose, -WarningAction, and -WarningVariable. For more information, see about_CommonParameters.

docs.title: RELATED LINKS
docs.text: Set-MOTDConfig

docs.title: SYNOPSIS
docs.text: Saves the persisted PSMotd cadence configuration.

docs.title: SYNTAX
docs.text:
Set-MOTDConfig [[-Frequency] <String>] [[-Scope] <String>] [-WhatIf]
 [-Confirm] [<CommonParameters>]

docs.title: DESCRIPTION
docs.text:
Set-MOTDConfig updates the saved cadence that Get-MOTD uses to decide whether a message of the day is due. This command only manages cadence persistence. To customize the content itself, define Get-MessageOfTheDay in your PowerShell profile.

User scope is the default. Machine and Enterprise scope writes may require an elevated PowerShell session.

docs.title: EXAMPLE 1
docs.text:
PS C:\> Set-MOTDConfig -Frequency Daily

Configures PSMotd to show once per calendar day.

docs.title: EXAMPLE 2
docs.text:
PS C:\> Set-MOTDConfig -Frequency Never

Disables automatic message-of-the-day output.

docs.title: EXAMPLE 3
docs.text:
PS C:\> Set-MOTDConfig -Frequency Weekly -Scope Machine

Saves the cadence for the machine-wide configuration store.

docs.title: -Frequency
docs.text:
Sets how often PSMotd should render automatically. Valid values are Never, EverySession, Daily, and Weekly.

Type: String
Parameter Sets: (All)
Aliases:

Required: False
Position: 1
Default value: None
Accept pipeline input: False
Accept wildcard characters: False

docs.title: -Scope
docs.text:
Selects which Configuration-module scope stores the settings. User is the default. Machine and Enterprise usually require elevation.

Type: String
Parameter Sets: (All)
Aliases:

Required: False
Position: 2
Default value: User
Accept pipeline input: False
Accept wildcard characters: False

docs.title: RELATED LINKS
docs.text:
Get-MOTDConfig

Get-MOTD

docs.title: SHORT DESCRIPTION
docs.text: PSMotd shows a message of the day in your PowerShell profile on the cadence you choose.

docs.title: LONG DESCRIPTION
docs.text:
PSMotd is a small profile helper for people who want useful startup information without turning every shell launch into noise.

The module does two separate jobs:

  • It decides when a message of the day is due.
  • It renders the content for that message.

Cadence is stored through the Configuration module. Use Set-MOTDConfig to pick Never, EverySession, Daily, or Weekly. The default scope is User.

Content comes from your profile. If you define a Get-MessageOfTheDay function in your PowerShell profile, PSMotd runs that function when a message is due. If you do not define one, PSMotd falls back to the built-in banner from Get-DefaultMessageOfTheDay.

The built-in banner is intentionally simple. It shows the current machine, the current user, and the current date. It exists as a safe fallback, not as the main customization surface.

docs.title: EXAMPLES
docs.text:
Import-Module PSMotd
Set-MOTDConfig -Frequency Daily
Get-MOTD

Shows the message of the day at most once per calendar day.

function Get-MessageOfTheDay {
    "Today's focus: ship the boring fix first."
}

Import-Module PSMotd
Get-MOTD -Now

Uses your profile-defined Get-MessageOfTheDay function and renders it immediately.

docs.title: NOTE
docs.text: Set-MOTDConfig controls when a message is shown. It does not store or execute custom message content.
